summary: Completed testing of the auto-detect bug fix. The critical bug where creating a case with 'Auto-detect' (empty state/offence_category) would default to NSW/Homicide has been FIXED. Backend tests confirm: (1) Case creation with empty state/offence_category results in None values, NOT nsw/homicide, (2) Explicit state/offence_category values are preserved, (3) Document upload triggers background auto-detection that populates case metadata from document content via LLM.
critical_code_review_comments: VERIFIED FIX: CaseCreate model (lines 214-215) correctly defines state: Optional[StateType] = None and offence_category: Optional[OffenceCategoryType] = None
VERIFIED FIX: Frontend handleCreateCase (lines 128-134) strips empty strings from payload so backend receives undefined for empty fields
VERIFIED: Document upload triggers _background_auto_detect_metadata which uses LLM to extract state, offence_category, offence_type, sentence, court from document content
VERIFIED: Background task only runs if content_text > 200 characters (line 228-229 in documents.py)
VERIFIED: Auto-detection validates against valid_cats and valid_sts lists before updating case

success_rate.frontend: N/A - App uses Emergent Google Auth which requires OAuth flow that cannot be automated in Playwright

test_credentials.note: Email/password login works via API but frontend uses Emergent Google Auth

seed_data_creation: Test cases created and cleaned up during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: The auto-detect bug fix has been verified. Case creation with empty state/offence_category now correctly results in None values (not nsw/homicide). Document upload triggers background LLM task that auto-detects metadata - wait 20-30 seconds after upload for task to complete. The app uses Emergent Google Auth so UI testing requires Google OAuth flow.

document_upload_auto_detection.description: Upload document with QLD armed robbery content
document_upload_auto_detection.expected_result: Background task populates state, offence_category, offence_type, sentence, court

bug_fix_verification.original_bug: Creating a case with 'Auto-detect' (leaving state and offence_category empty) would default to NSW/Homicide
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Made state and offence_category Optional[None] in Pydantic models. Frontend strips empty strings from payload.
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: FIXED - Case creation with empty fields now results in state=None, offence_category=None
